question: For flow of petrol from fuel tank to the carburettor, the fuel system includes
explanation: A mechanical or electric fuel pump is required to deliver fuel from the tank to the carburetor, especially when the tank is lower than the engine.
subject: Automotive Fuel Systems
ai_correct: Because fuel tanks are often located lower than or at a distance from the carburettor (especially in rear-tank layouts), a mechanical or electrical fuel pump is required to positively supply petrol from the fuel tank to the carburettor float bowl.
ai_wrong: Students might mistakenly think gravity or a vacuum intake alone (Options A and B) suffices, ignoring that modern fuel delivery systems require a dedicated fuel pump for continuous pressure and flow.
formula: \Delta P_{fuel} \text{ supplied by Mechanical/Electric Fuel Pump}
trick: Whenever fuel needs to be transferred reliably against gravitational or distance gradients from tank to carburettor, a fuel pump is used.
